items.title: Hundreds protest against Pauline Hanson as WA Labor seats on 'hit list'

children.content: A crowd of roughly 400 protesters demonstrates against One Nation's Pauline Hanson as she speaks at a sundowner event in Perth's eastern suburbs.

gallery.altText: A group of protesters holding anti-Pauline Hanson signs.
A blurred image of a car with red paint reading "F*** pauline". Near a protest against Pauline Hanson in Midland.

thumbnail.altText: A group of protesters holding anti-Pauline Hanson signs.
A blurred image of a car with red paint reading "F*** pauline". Near a protest against Pauline Hanson in Midland.

items.title: Fisherman attacked after treading on crocodile in northern WA

image.altText: Mangroves and creek with a blue esky on shore

children.content: Authorities have confirmed the 35-year-old man was fishing in waist deep water at Barred Creek, north of Broome, when he was bitten.

items.title: Why Bunnings moving a store 10km down the road has caused such a stir

image.altText: The exterior of a large Bunnings Warehouse.

children.content: A plan by hardware giant Bunnings move its Bunbury store 10km to a neighbouring shire has angered and concerned residents and businesses.

items.title: Person dies after contracting rare mosquito-borne disease in WA

children.content: One person has died after contracting Murray Valley Encephalitis in Western Australia's Kimberley region.

gallery.altText: A close-up of a mosquito on skin, against a blue background.

thumbnail.altText: A close-up of a mosquito on skin, against a blue background.

items.title: John Worsfold, Gary Ablett Jr added to Australian Football Hall of Fame

children.content: Six new members are inducted into the Australian Football Hall of Fame, including West Coast premiership captain and coach John Worsfold and Geelong and Gold Coast great Gary Ablett Jr.

gallery.altText: John Worsfold poses with his Australian Football Hall of Fame inductee certificate.
Gary Ablett Jr in a tuxedo seen side-on with his name on a screen behind him.

thumbnail.altText: John Worsfold poses with his Australian Football Hall of Fame inductee certificate.
Gary Ablett Jr in a tuxedo seen side-on with his name on a screen behind him.

items.title: Notre Dame university halts new nursing student enrolments for another year

children.content: The university says it will not take new students for its nursing program for another year, as it needs to "rebalance enrolments across the program within accredited program capacity".

image.altText: A sign for the University of Notre Dame at its Fremantle campus

items.title: Damning report finds WA prisoners subjected to 'cruel, degrading treatment'

children.content: WA prisoners are being subjected to "cruel, inhumane or degrading treatment" because the state government has allowed prisons to become "almost fundamentally broken", according to the Inspector of Custodial Services.

items.title: Why was Rolf Harris never tried for his crimes in Australia?

children.content: Rolf Harris's conviction for child sexual abuse was shocking to those who saw him as a lovable larrikin. But the people who were abused by him are refusing to stay silent about the impacts of his crimes.

gallery.altText: Black and white photo of a young Rolf Harris in a woolen jumper and thick glasses looking over his shoulder and smiling

thumbnail.altText: Black and white photo of a young Rolf Harris in a woolen jumper and thick glasses looking over his shoulder and smiling

items.title: 'When Neale spoke, people felt it': Daniher farewelled at MCG funeral

children.content: Family, friends, members of the AFL community and dignitaries attend the service to remember the AFL legend, MND campaigner and former Australia of the Year.

image.altText: A painting of a man between the Australian and Aboriginal flag above a coffin draped in the Australian flag and native flowers.

items.title: 'It's an unprecedented matter': Bondi gunman hit with 19 more charges

children.content: Akram is facing charges of murder and committing a terrorist act over the attack at Bondi Beach on 14 December.

items.title: Former WA Police officer 'gutted' after compensation for horrific injury denied

children.content: Former WA Police officer and WA Australian of the Year Paul Litherland says he is "heartbroken" after his claim for post-service medical compensation over injuries he received in the force was denied.

items.title: Incorrect Medicare billing allegations spark concerns of broader issue

children.content: One of Australia's largest medical imaging providers, Integral Diagnostics, says it will look into allegations of improper Medicare billing at a regional WA public hospital where the company provides radiology services.

image.altText: A hand holds a medicare card

items.title: Judge throws out appeals over homophobic tweet judgement

children.content: A Federal Court judge has dismissed an appeal against a judgement which ordered New South Wales MP Mark Latham to pay $140,000 to independent MP Alex Greenwich over a homophobic tweet in 2023.

image.altText: Mark Latham wearing a suit speaking to media on the street outside court.

items.title: New leader, old problem for WA Liberals as Zempilas flirts with One Nation

children.content: As Basil Zempilas flirts with One Nation, will the right-wing populist party drag the WA Liberals down again, or will it become a life raft on the way to a political resurrection?

articles.title: Ignoring One Nation like 'ignoring will of people', Basil Zempilas warns

children.content: WA Opposition Leader Basil Zempilas says One Nation deserves "respect" and he is open to finding a way of working with the right-wing populist party as support for it grows.

image.altText: Basil Zempilas stands at a podium and addresses the crowd

gallery.altText: A close up of the torso of a man in a suit surrounded by people
A woman speaks to the camera
A man in a navy cap wears a bright orange shirt with the words  "We've got the guts to say what you're thinking" on the back.

thumbnail.altText: A close up of the torso of a man in a suit surrounded by people
A woman speaks to the camera
A man in a navy cap wears a bright orange shirt with the words  "We've got the guts to say what you're thinking" on the back.

items.title: Residents shocked by severe storm as South West WA homes left flooded

children.content: An unexpected deluge floods homes and public facilities in the South West WA town of Harvey, with the community pelted with 40 millimetres of rain in one hour.

gallery.altText: Flooding in a street in the town of Harvey
A wheelie bin floating in water over a residential road
Picture of a flooded driveway in Harvey

thumbnail.altText: Flooding in a street in the town of Harvey
A wheelie bin floating in water over a residential road
Picture of a flooded driveway in Harvey

items.title: Too poor to leave Australia. These people are trapped with no safety net

children.content: A man dies. His body is not noticed for nearly a week. He was invisible to many, like the growing number of homeless Australia wide.

image.altText: A homeless person lays on a makeshift bed as commuters pass him by.

items.title: Thirteen government agencies embroiled in potential data breach

children.content: Thirteen government agencies are now embroiled in an expanding scandal involving potential data breaches prompting calls for an urgent audit amid concerns over national security.

image.altText: A stack of documents sitting in an office, generic.

items.title: From the Kimberley to Sweden, how heartbreak helped me take a travel leap

children.content: As I approached the age of 30, I longed to travel outside of Australia, but there was always an excuse or obstacle. A chance to share my culture with the world finally got me there.

gallery.altText: Molly in front of some of her art

thumbnail.altText: Molly in front of some of her art

items.title: O'Callaghan chasing Titmus and 'the impossible' as she misses world record

children.content: Mollie O'Callaghan shoots out of the gates in the 200m freestyle but cannot maintain the world record pace as she books her ticket to the Glasgow Commonwealth Games.

image.altText: Mollie O'Callaghan in the water after the 200m freestyle.

items.title: Shaggy dog tale as Bruce rescued after being blown out to sea in kayak

children.content: Bruce, a dog, has been rescued from the North Sea after drifting away in an inflatable kayak.

image.altText: A large shaggy black dog with grey flecks on a nest of blankets.

items.title: When does the FIFA World Cup start and who are the favourites?

children.content: The FIFA World Cup is the biggest single-sport event on the planet and has delivered numerous historical moments, from Diego Maradona's "hand of God" in 1986 to Zinedine Zidane's infamous headbutt on Marco Materazzi.

articles.title: Schwarzer confident 'written off' Socceroos can stun American doubters

children.content: Maligned, underestimated and overlooked — and that is just the way we like it according to the Socceroos' most capped player.

image.altText: Two men on a panel show, in a stadium, holding microphones

articles.title: FIFA prioritising profit over fans at World Cup, ex-official says

children.content: A former FIFA official says the upcoming World Cup has been tainted by corporate greed as two US states investigate ticket pricing and practices.

image.altText: Wearing a soccer jersey, Miguel Medina sits by a soccer field

articles.title: Will you be in North America for the FIFA World Cup? We want to hear from you

children.content: If you're an Aussie fan making the trip, or already there, share your experience with us.

thumbnail.altText: A smiling man lifts a golden trophy among celebrating teammates with fireworks exploding in the background.
